data.title: Trump is mainly targeting China with metals tariffs, former trade adviser says
data.description:
President Donald Trump’s tariffs on aluminum and steel imports are mostly driven by concerns that China is glutting global markets and hurting U.S. manufacturing, a former trade adviser to the White House said.

The president this month signed an executive order to place 25% levies on all imports of steel and aluminum from any source. He said the tariffs, due to take effect on March 12, were needed to support U.S. production and to create jobs.

The order is “a reflection of this worry that what the U.S. has done thus far has not been sufficient to stop overcapacity in these sectors,” Kate Kalutkiewicz, senior director for international trade policy at the National Economic Council during Trump’s first term, said to the Canadian Broadcasting Corp.

“They almost entirely relate to China, and China’s non-market policies and practices which have enabled this global glut,” she said.

data.title: Credit card delinquencies, net charge-offs hold relatively stable in January
data.description:
In the first month of 2025, credit card metrics held roughly stable, as the labor market remains solid and household balance sheets are generally still healthy. Lending activity slowed during the month, with loans falling 2.7% to $512B, the sum of eight banks, from $526.2B in the previous month.

On average, the credit card delinquency ticked up to 3.07% from 2.90% in December, while the net charge-off rate eased ever so slightly to 4.29% from 4.31% during the month. But the average was heavily influenced by a significant month-over-month improvement in Bread Financial Holding's (NYSE:BFH) delinquency and charge-off rates. Excluding Bread, the average delinquency rate rose to 2.63% from 2.40% in the prior month, and the average net charge-off rate climbed to 3.78% from 3.68%.

The data looked more encouraging when looking at pace of change. "All card names reported faster Y/Y declines in delinquency rates, with the average now down -25bp Y/Y vs prior -13bp Y/Y," Morgan Stanley analyst Jeffrey Adelson wrote in a note to clients.

He affirmed that the standout in January was Bread Financial (NYSE:BFH), which had the largest beat on net charge-offs vs. Morgan Stanley's estimate. "Losses & delinquencies both outperformed seasonality, with faster Y/Y declines," Adelson noted.

The underperformer was American Express (NYSE:AXP), he said, with consumer and small business net charge-offs falling short of Morgan Stanley estimates and January seasonality, and reaccelerating Y/Y.

However, loan growth was slower than he expected, at an average of ~3.3% Y/Y, led by Bread Financial (BFH), down 190 basis points to -2% Y/Y.

data.title: Merz likely to be next German leader after Scholz concedes election loss
data.description:
Friedrich Merz is likely to become Germany’s next leader after his conservative party topped the voting in national elections held Sunday, according to exit polls.

Chancellor Olaf Scholz conceded defeat. 

According to media reports, the exit polls showed that voters rejected Sholz’s Social Democratic Party’s handling of the economy and immigration.

The New York Times reported that Merz’s Christian Democratic Party and sister party Christian Social Union lead the voting with a combined 29%. The Social Democrats had 16%, and the right week AfD received 19%.

data.title: American Airlines flight diverted to Rome after bomb threat: reports
data.description:
An American Airlines (NASDAQ:AAL) flight from New York to New Delhi safely landed in Rome after the crew reported a security issue, the Federal Aviation Administration said on Sunday.

The FAA did not specify what the security issue was. However, according to X account Flight Emergency, the flight was forced to land in Italy due to a bomb threat.

"American flight AA292 will divert to Rome en route to Delhi due to a bomb threat," said flight-tracking account Flight Emergency in a post on X.

The Boeing 787-9 Dreamliner flight with about 200 people onboard landed at Rome's Leonardo da Vinci airport at about 16:20 GMT, Reuters reported. Flight 292 was over the Caspian Sea when the airline warned the crew of a bomb threat, the report said.

A senior official briefed on the matter was cited by ABC News as saying that a bomb threat was received by email but deemed to be unfounded.

The flight had departed from John F. Kennedy International Airport in New York and was traveling to Indira Gandhi International Airport in New Delhi, India.

"American Airlines flight 292, with service from New York (JFK) to Delhi (DEL), diverted to Rome (FCO) due to a possible security concern," said American Airlines (AAL) in a statement. "The flight landed safely at FCO, and law enforcement inspected and cleared the aircraft to re-depart."

More on American Airlines

data.title: 'Captain America: Brave New World' crashes in second weekend
data.description:
Marvel’s “Captain America: Brave New World” fell back to earth during its second weekend, bringing in $28.2M this past weekend at the box office, falling from its $100M from its debut during the four-day holiday weekend.

It still remained at No. 1 from Friday through Sunday, according to studio estimates.

Horror movie “The Monkey” opened with $14.2M to take the second position. “Paddington in Peru” made $6.5M to take No. 3 during its second weekend.

“Dog Man” finished fourth with $5.9M, and “Ne Zha 2” rounded out the top 5 with $3.1M.

The top 10 weekend movies, according to Comscore are:

1. “Captain America: Brave New World,” $28.2M.

2. “The Monkey,” $14.2M.

3. “Paddington in Peru,” $6.5M.

4. “Dog Man,” $5.9M.

5. “Ne Zha 2,” $3.1M.

6. “Heart Eyes,” $2.9M.

7. “Mufasa: The Lion King,” $2.5M.

8. “The Unbreakable Boy,” $2.5M.

9. “Chhaava,” $1.5M.

10. “One of Them Days,” $1.4M.

data.title: Eyes on Austin: Tesla will take on Waymo and Uber in an early robotaxi battle
data.description:
Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) is set to launch its first autonomous robotaxi service in Austin, Texas in June. The city is seen as ideal for Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) because it is now the location of the company's headquarters, and autonomous vehicles can operate on public roads in Texas with registration and insurance but do not require special permits.

Tesla's robotaxi service features vehicles operating with human drivers using the company's "unsupervised" Full Self-Driving technology. Initially, the robotaxi fleet will consist of Tesla-owned vehicles, likely Model 3 and Model Y. Customers will use a mobile app to order an autonomous ride. The ordered robotaxis will be able to park in designated areas for convenient pickups.

Tesla (TSLA) plans a gradual rollout in specific areas of Austin as it monitors safety issues. The company will collaborate with Austin city officials and first responders as more robotaxis are added to the fleet.

Austin’s Transportation and Public Works Department said in an emailed statement that it works with Tesla (TSLA) and other autonomous vehicle companies individually as they make plans to enter the market. To that end, the department has shared maps of schools and school zones, details on traffic control for special events, and information on fire and police emergency service vehicles and procedures to assist with the launch process.

Looking further ahead, a national rollout in the U.S. is targeted for 2026 if the company lands the required regulatory approvals in a timely fashion. The company's purpose-built Cyber cab is anticipated to feature prominently. Texas has more lenient regulations for autonomous vehicles compared to other states.

The Austin test will be a key investment story in 2025 as the safety and reliability of the FSD systems is evaluated. There will also be important reads on demand, insurance considerations, and the issue of liability if any accidents occur.

Tesla (TSLA) is not the only company with plans for a robotaxi service. Alphabet's (NASDAQ:GOOG) (GOOGL) Waymo One is up and running in Phoenix, San Francisco, and Los Angeles, with Austin, Atlanta, and Miami also being targeted for the service. Amazon's Zoox (Zoox), Lyft (LYFT), Uber Technologies (UBER), Aurora Innovation (AUR), May Mobilty, and Motional (APTV) (OTCPK:HYMTF) also have U.S. robotaxi goals through parternships or upcoming initiatives. Globally, some of the major robotaxi players include Baidu (BIDU) Apollo Go, Pony AI (PONY), WeRide (WRD), XPeng (XPEV), BYD Company (OTCPK:BYDDF), and AutoX.

Interestingly, Uber Technologies (UBER) does not appear to be on track to partner with Tesla (TSLA) in the early stages, according to Chief Executive Officer Dara Khosrowshahi, who said he has had direct conversations with Elon Musk on the issue. "They want to build it alone, so to some extent in Austin, we and Waymo will be competing with Tesla when they launch," he noted last week at an industry conference. "Life is long, but we would love to partner with them," he added.

data.title: Here are top-rated defensive stocks as market rally sputters
data.description:
Industries that typically hold up well during times of stock-market volatility – such as healthcare, consumer staples and utilities – may be worth another look as the rally sputters.

U.S. stocks on Friday posted their worst day of the year as soft economic data led investors to re-price the market for risks. The Standard & Poor’s 500 (SP500) stock index fell 1.7% for its biggest single-day drop since mid-December.

Stagflation – a term to describe high inflation amid meager economic growth – is especially worrisome as a University of Michigan survey last week showed that consumers expect prices to rise faster than they did before, while the S&P Global U.S. services purchasing managers’ index fell to a 25-month low to indicate a slowdown in business activity.

Seeking Alpha offers a variety of screening tools to help identify stocks with the strongest collective value, growth, profitability, EPS revisions and price momentum compared with peers. data.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy says he's prepared to resign for NATO membership
data.description:
Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Sunday he is “ready” to step down if it would bring peace and possibly NATO membership.

"If there is peace for Ukraine, if you really need me to leave my post, I am ready. ... I can exchange it for NATO,” he said in response to a question at a news conference in Kyiv.

Zelensky said he could quit “immediately,” if needed.

The comments came amid rising tensions between Zelenskyy and U.S. President Donald Trump, who falsely accused the Ukrainian leader of starting the war with Russia in 2022. Trump then accused Zelenskyy of being a “dictator” after he said the U.S. leader was in a Russian “disinformation space.”

Monday is the third anniversary of Russia’s invasion of Ukraine.

data.title: Boeing's safety head is set to retire, internal memo says
data.description:
Boeing's (NYSE:BA) chief aerospace safety officer, Mike Delaney, is set to retire this year, Reuters reported Friday, citing a company memo.

The aerospace and defense giant company in the next few weeks will name Delaney’s successor, Chief Executive Kelly Ortberg said in the message.

Delaney has worked at Boeing (NYSE:BA) for nearly four decades. In 2021, he was appointed to oversee the company’s safety practices.

"His contributions over the decades have advanced our global aviation safety strategy, helped our industry safely resume global air travel during the pandemic and defined how we will develop a future generation of commercial airplanes," Ortberg said in the message.

Aviation authorities have scrutinized Boeing’s (BA) safety practices after several incidents, including the midflight blowout of a door panel from a new 737 Max 9 flown by Alaska Airlines in January 2024.

data.title: Bangladesh invites Elon Musk to visit, launch Starlink service
data.description:
Bangladesh's interim leader, Muhammad Yunus, invited SpaceX (SPACE) boss Elon Musk to visit the country and launch its Starlink satellite service there in 90 working days.

The satellite-based internet service will mainly benefit Bangladesh's young men and women, Yunus' office said in a post on X on Sunday, citing a letter he sent to the billionaire on February 19.

Yunus held a lengthy video call with Musk on Thursday to discuss ways to bring Starlink to Bangladesh, as the country's fragile interim government seeks U.S. diplomatic support.

Musk is yet to publicly comment on the meeting or the letter.

More on SpaceX
